title: Iterative decoder for decoding a code composed of at least two constraint nodes
abstract: An iterative decoder configured for decoding a code having a codeword length N, comprises: â¢ N variable nodes (VNs) v n , n = 1...N, configured to receive a LLR I n defined on a alphabet A I of q ch quantization bits, q ch â¥2; â¢ M constraint nodes (CNs) c m , m = 1...M, 2 â¤ M < N; â¢ v n and c m exchanging messages along edges of a Tanner graph; â¢ each v n sending messages m vn â cm to c m , the set of connected constraint nodes being noted V (vn) , and V (vn)\{cm} being V (vn) except c m , and, â¢ each c m sending messages m cm â vn to v n ; â¢ the LLR I n and the messages m vn â cm and m cmâvn are coded according to a sign-and-magnitude code; and â¢ each variable node v n , for each iteration l , compute: â¢ sign-preserving factors: (formula I) where Î¾ is a positive or a null integer; â¢(formula II) and (formula III) where S is a function from the set of value that can take floor (formula IV) to the set A s .
claims:
Claims 1. Iterative decoder configured for decoding a code composed of at least two constraint nodes and having a codeword length N, said decoder 5 comprising: â¢ N variable nodes (VNs) v n , n = each variable node v n being configured to receive a log-likelihood ratio LLR l n of the channel decoded bit n of the codeword to be decoded, said LLR l n being defined on a alphabet AL of q Ch quantization bits, q Ch being an integer 10 equal or greater than 2; â¢ M constraint nodes (CNs) c m , m = 1...M, 2 < M < N ; â¢ the variable nodes and the constraint nodes being the nodes of a Tanner graph; variable nodes and constraint nodes being configured to exchange messages along edges of the Tanner graph; 15 â¢ each variable node v n being configured for estimating the value y n of the n th bit of the codeword to be decoded and for sending messages m VnÂ®Cm , messages belonging to an alphabet As of q quantization bits, q Â£ q Ch > to the connected constraint nodes c m , the set of connected constraint nodes being noted V (vn) , defining the degree d v of the 20 variable node v n as the size of the set V( vn) , and V( vn)\{cm} being the set V (vn) except the constraint node c m , and then, â¢ each constraint nodes c m being configured for testing the received message contents with predetermined constraints and sending messages m C nÂ®V i , messages belonging to an alphabet A c , to the 25 connected variable nodes v n ; â¢ the message emissions being repeated until the decoding of the codeword is achieved successfully or a predetermined number of iteration is reached; and â¢ the LLR I n and the messages m VnÂ®Cm and m CmÂ®Vn are coded according 30 to a sign-and-magnitude code in the alphabets AL, A S and Ac 

 symmetric around zero, AL = {-N qCh , ... , -1 , -0, +0, +1 , ...,+N qCh }, A s = {- N q> ..., -1 , -0, +0, +1 , ...,+N q }, Ac = {-N q ,..â -1 , -0, +0, +1 , ...,+N q } in which the sign indicates the estimated bit value and the magnitude represents its reliability; and 5 â¢ each variable node v n , for each iteration l, is configured to compute: â¢ sign-preserving factors, sum of the ponderated sign of LLR l n and the sum of the signs of all messages, except for the message coming from c m , received from the connected constraint nodes at iteration /: positive or a null integer; â¢ messages to connected constraint nodes c m for iteration l +1 computed in two steps function from the set of value that can take floor to the set As. 2. The iterative decoder of claim 1 wherein x equal to 0 if d v = 2, equal to 1 20 if d v > 2 and d v is odd, and equal to 2 if d v > 2 and d v is even. 3. The iterative decoder of claim 1 or 2, wherein the constraints applied by the constraint nodes are parity check, convolution code or any block code. 25 4. The iterative decoder of claim 3, wherein the codeword is coded by LDPC and the constraint nodes are check nodes. 5. The iterative decoder of any preceding claim, wherein the function S is defined as S(x) = min(max( x - l(c), 0),+Nq), where l(c) is an integer offset value depending on the value of x. 5 6. The iterative decoder of claim 5, wherein l(c) is a random variable that can take it value in a predefined set of values according to a predefined law. 7. The iterative decoder of any preceding claim, wherein alphabets A s and 10 Ac are identical. 8. The iterative decoder of any preceding claim wherein the means comprises â¢ at least one processor; and 15 â¢ at least one memory including computer program code, the at least one memory and computer program code configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the performance of the decoder. 9. An iterative decoding method for decoding a code composed of at least 20 two constraint nodes and having a codeword length N, by an iterative decoder according to any one of claims 1 to 8, said method comprising: â¢ reception and storage (21 ) of the LLR l n by the variable node vn, n = 1 ...N; â¢ emission (23) of messages m V iÂ®Cm , 25 â¢ verification (25) of the constraints by the constraint nodes c m ; â¢ emission (27) of messages m CmÂ®Vn containing a possible candidate value for the variable n solving the constraints; â¢ computation (29) by the variable nodes of + where x is a positive or a null integer, and 

 of messages to connected constraint nodes c m for iteration l +1 computed in two steps 5 function from the set of value that can take floor to the set As and. â¢ iteration from emission of messages m VnÂ®Cm to their computation for the next iteration until the codeword is decoded or the number of iteration reaches a predetermined count. 1 0 10. A computer readable medium encoding a machine-executable program of instructions to perform a method according to claim 9.
